title: Corrective fix for B-4ad21cc2-test: Test Suppress duplicate auto-followups while preserving legacy behavior
description:
Blocked reason: `uv run python -m unittest tests.test_orchestrator.DeleteBeadCliTests.test_scheduler_uses_planner_owned_shared_followups_without_creating_legacy_children -v` fails because legacy followup beads are still created (`AssertionError` at tests/test_orchestrator.py:262).

Remaining work: Update the scheduler followup-selection logic so developer beads inside planner-created feature trees reuse existing shared tester/documentation/review beads instead of creating legacy child followups, then rerun `uv run python -m unittest tests.test_orchestrator -v` or at minimum the failing followup test plus nearby scheduler followup coverage.
acceptance_criteria: Implement the minimum fix required to unblock B-4ad21cc2-test.
Update tests/docs as needed for the corrective change.
Leave a handoff summary that states how the parent bead can be retried.

handoff_summary.completed: Implemented the corrective fix in scheduler followup creation logic to avoid backfilling legacy `-test`/`-docs`/`-review` children when planner-owned shared followups already exist for the developer bead. Verified the updated module compiles with `uv run python -m py_compile src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py`.
handoff_summary.remaining: Tester agent should retry the previously failing scheduler followup coverage, at minimum `tests.test_orchestrator.DeleteBeadCliTests.test_scheduler_uses_planner_owned_shared_followups_without_creating_legacy_children`, and ideally the nearby scheduler followup tests referenced in the bead description.
handoff_summary.risks: No test suite was run per guardrails, so the change is syntax-checked only. The logic now treats the presence of any planner-owned shared followup as authoritative and skips legacy backfill for that developer bead; downstream validation should confirm that partial shared-followup setups are still the intended behavior.
handoff_summary.next_action: Retry the failing scheduler followup test coverage against the updated scheduler logic, then proceed with the parent bead if green.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Low. The change stays isolated to `src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py`, but downstream edits should preserve the distinction between planner-owned shared followups and legacy/manual per-developer followups.

conflict_risks: Low. The change stays isolated to `src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py`, but downstream edits should preserve the distinction between planner-owned shared followups and legacy/manual per-developer followups.

execution_history.summary: Deferred due to file-scope conflict with active bead B-4ad21cc2-docs
Deferred due to file-scope conflict with active bead B-d4ab70d3

Adjusted scheduler followup selection so planner-owned shared followups are checked separately and any shared tester/documentation/review bead in a planner-created feature tree suppresses legacy per-developer auto-followup creation. Standalone/manual developer beads still fall back to legacy child followups.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
